stack: Android APK (decompiled) — IPC / deep links: intent redirection, exported auth-bypass, content-provider traversal (rung 4 of the mobile ladder)

vulns.desc: DeepLinkActivity is exported + BROWSABLE (dlforge://go) and takes the 'target' query parameter from the inbound link, calls Intent.parseUri(target) and startActivity() with NO validation — classic intent redirection: an attacker (or any web page) can redirect the app into an unexported internal Activity (auth bypass) or out to an attacker-chosen component/target.
vulns.exploit: am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d 'dlforge://go?target=intent:...' ; or a web link dlforge://go?target=<intent-uri>.

vulns.desc: FilesProvider is an exported ContentProvider whose openFile() builds a File from the request URI's last path segment appended raw to the files dir, with no canonicalization or '../' check — a path-traversal that lets any app read arbitrary files in the app sandbox (databases, shared_prefs, private files) via content://com.dlforge.app.files/.. .
vulns.exploit: content read --uri 'content://com.dlforge.app.files/..%2Fdatabases%2Fwallet.db' from any app -> arbitrary sandbox file read.

vulns.desc: DashboardActivity is the authenticated account screen but is android:exported="true" with no permission and performs no auth check of its own (it assumes it is reached only after login). Any installed app can launch it directly, bypassing the login flow.
vulns.exploit: am start -n com.dlforge.app/.DashboardActivity -> account screen shown without authenticating.
